qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via unbounded file uploads on public webhook endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets multipart parsing on public webhook endpoints through unbounded formidable file handling, not n8n expression evaluation sandbox escape or crafted workflow-parameter expressions.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url and an unsafe axios.get(query.url) server-side fetch, not the expression sandbox escape that lets crafted workflow parameter expressions execute host commands.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described n8n expression evaluation sandbox escape or equivalent crafted-expression source-to-command-execution path. The final qualifying findings cover a workflow import SSRF and public webhook multipart upload resource exhaustion, which are different endpoints, code paths, and root causes.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-vpcf-gvg4-6qwr describes authenticated workflow creators/modifiers abusing crafted expressions in workflow parameters to trigger unintended host command execution.
ssrf-workflow-import/vulnerability_report.json identifies GET /rest/workflows/from-url and axios.get(query.url) in workflows.controller.ts as an SSRF root cause.
dos-webhook-multipart/vulnerability_report.json identifies packages/cli/src/webhooks/webhook-form-data.ts formidable settings on public webhook endpoints as an unbounded upload DoS root cause.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists only dos-webhook-multipart and ssrf-workflow-import as final qualifying vulnerability folders.
